_meta.role: CX Learning & Development — Facilitation & Programs Manager
_meta.what_firm_does: Gusto is a payroll, benefits, and HR platform built for small and growing businesses. Mission: create a world where work empowers a better life, starting by helping small businesses run payroll and take care of their teams.
_meta.jd_wants: Own and facilitate cohort-based L&D programs for the Customer Experience org end-to-end — diagnose capability gaps, design and deliver training at scale, and refine programs with data until outcomes are measurable.
_meta.status: DRAFT — Jamie to review/edit before submit

Why are you interested in this role?: Learning & development isn't just about delivering a great session—from my experience, it's a continuous cycle of diagnosing where capability gaps actually live, designing cohort experiences that close them, and refining with data until the outcomes are measurable. That full loop is the work I already do. At InGenius Prep I own the end-to-end implementation of 20+ learning programs, facilitate live cohort training and webinars for 600+ participants at a 78% enrollment rate, and run needs assessments with 25 global stakeholders to find the real gaps before designing anything. A CX L&D programs role is that exact rhythm, pointed at a customer-facing team—diagnose, build, facilitate, measure. That's the center of what I enjoy, not the edge of it.
Why do you want to work at Gusto?: The mission is a real draw—Gusto growing the small business economy, and the fact that you're investing in your CX team's capability rather than treating it as a cost center. That signals People and learning are seen as strategic, which is the environment I do my best work in. And the role fits how I'm built: I'm an analytical organizer who treats L&D as data-informed first. At NextGen Healthcare I translated 2,000+ responses into reports that shaped L&D strategy, which deepened my conviction that strong programs start with evidence, not a content calendar. I'd be glad to bring that diagnose-first instinct to a team whose whole job is taking care of customers.
What excites you about this opportunity?: The chance to build facilitation and programs that genuinely travel and stick. At Vestas I piloted an Inclusive Leadership workshop for 12 senior leaders that later scaled to 23 global locations—early proof that I can design leadership-development experiences that hold up beyond the room they started in. Doing that for a CX org at Gusto's scale, where the learning directly shapes how customers get supported, is the part that energizes me. I like the measurement discipline of it too: not just running a cohort, but tracking whether capability actually moved. Building something that levels up a whole team, and being able to prove it did, is what I'd get up for.
Tell us about a time you designed a lear: At InGenius Prep, onboarding for 70+ staff was slow and inconsistent, so new hires took too long to get productive. I ran needs assessments with stakeholders to pinpoint where people actually got stuck, then designed cross-functional documentation, guides, and cohort-based training—increasingly built with AI tools—to close those specific gaps. The result was onboarding time cut by 75%, alongside a 78% program enrollment rate across the broader portfolio. What made it work wasn't a polished deck; it was diagnosing the real friction first and then tracking whether the fix held. That's the throughline I'd bring to Gusto's CX learning programs—design for the measured gap, then prove the learning stuck.
